# Proposal Outline  - B-2622LM On Demand Pressure Washing, Softwash & Window Cleaning

_Generated: 2026-06-05T05:15:12.458Z (deterministic)_

## Executive Summary
Thrare Contracting submits this proposal in response to Gwinnett County Public Schools's requirement for B-2622LM On Demand Pressure Washing, Softwash & Window Cleaning. As a certified DBE/SBA/Minority-Owned Small Business, Thrare Contracting brings proven capability in the required services with $529,200+ in past contract value. Our approach emphasizes rapid mobilization, documented quality control, and direct owner oversight  - Eric White, Owner/PM, serves as the single point of contact for all contract activities. We have identified 1 high/critical compliance requirements and address each directly in this proposal. Price competitively as lowest responsive bidder (eRFQ/IFB evaluation).

## Technical Approach
Thrare Contracting will execute all pressure washing services using a three-phase approach: pre-wash assessment (surface type, staining severity, access constraints), chemical application calibrated to substrate (concrete, masonry, membrane roofing), and high-pressure rinse with containment of wastewater per EPA stormwater guidelines. Scope includes Tax Form: W-9. MBE/DBE participation goal. All work follows OSHA 29 CFR 1926 safety standards with crew-specific JSA documentation. Equipment includes commercial-grade 4,000 PSI units, soft-wash systems for delicate surfaces, and surface cleaners for large-area efficiency.

## Management Plan
Eric White, Owner/PM, serves as the single point of contact and Contractor Project Manager for all task orders. Weekly progress reports are submitted to the COR detailing completed tasks, upcoming schedule, and any issues. A change-control process handles scope modifications  - no work proceeds outside the PWS without written COR approval. MBE/DBE teaming advantage  - Rare Earth's minority certification adds value as either prime or team member. Consider joint venture if scope exceeds solo capacity. Risk mitigation addresses: No critical risk items detected  - review solicitation manually.

## Staffing and Key Personnel
Eric White, Owner/Project Manager (7+ years experience in facility services, recycling logistics, and project management) directs all operations and serves as the primary interface with the COR. Field crews consist of trained technicians with current OSHA 10-hour certifications and background checks where required. Staffing is scaled to the scope: Tax Form: W-9. MBE/DBE participation goal. All personnel are W-2 employees or vetted subcontractors with documented qualifications.

## Past Performance
Rare Earth Ltd. holds 4 contracts totaling $529,200+. While no contracts are an exact scope match, the following demonstrate transferable capability in project execution, logistics, and facility services: **Recycled Gravel & Aggregate Supply**  - Fulton County Government – Department of Public Works, $231,200, Prime Contractor, 2025-03–2026-03. Delivered 3,600+ tons; 100% on-time delivery; GDOT Compliance. **Recycling & Sustainability Project Management – Advisory, Management, Logistics & Implementation**  - Project Management Portfolio, $198,000, Senior Project Manager, 2025-03–2026-03. Managed logistics for large recycling flows; Coordinated multi-partner teams; Implemented Sustainability and Recycling Goals. **Recycling & Waste Management – Sustainability and Circular Economy Services**  - CheckSammy, $50,000, Subcontractor, ongoing–ongoing. Diverted 100+ tons of waste; Supported ESG tracking; Furthered recycling efforts throughout processes.

## Quality Control
Thrare Contracting implements a three-tier QC process: (1) pre-task planning with crew-specific checklists derived from the PWS, (2) in-progress inspection by the crew lead at defined checkpoints, and (3) post-task documentation including timestamped, GPS-tagged photos of completed work. Nonconformances trigger a corrective action cycle: identify deficiency → root cause → corrective action → COR notification → re-inspection within 24 hours. Customer satisfaction is verified through post-service follow-up.

## Pricing Narrative
Price competitively as lowest responsive bidder (eRFQ/IFB evaluation).

## Executive Summary
Rare Earth Ltd. submits this proposal in response to DEPT OF DEFENSE's requirement for GROUNDS MAINTENANCE FORT STEWART/HAAF. As a certified DBE/SBA/Minority-Owned Small Business, Rare Earth brings proven capability in pressure washing, pressure washing with $529,200+ in past contract value. Our approach emphasizes rapid mobilization, documented quality control, and direct owner oversight  - Eric White, Owner/PM, serves as the single point of contact for all contract activities. Price competitively as lowest responsive bidder (eRFQ/IFB evaluation).

## Technical Approach
Rare Earth Ltd. will perform grounds maintenance on a scheduled cycle covering mowing, edging, trimming, debris removal, and seasonal cleanup. Scope includes Solicitation: Evaluation. Pricing Form: and Locations List – Pressure Washing Services. E-Verify enrollment required. Our crew operates commercial-grade zero-turn mowers, string trimmers, and blowers. Seasonal services include leaf removal, storm debris cleanup, and bed maintenance. All clippings are mulched or removed per facility requirements.

## Management Plan
Eric White, Owner/PM, serves as the single point of contact and Contractor Project Manager for all task orders. Weekly progress reports are submitted to the COR detailing completed tasks, upcoming schedule, and any issues. A change-control process handles scope modifications  - no work proceeds outside the PWS without written COR approval. MBE/DBE teaming advantage  - Rare Earth's minority certification adds value as either prime or team member. Consider joint venture if scope exceeds solo capacity. Risk mitigation addresses: Insurance: 3 coverage types required  - Commercial General Liability (see amounts in doc), Automobile Liability (see amounts in doc), Workers Comp. Verify current certificates meet minimums.; Background checks required on all assigned personnel..

## Staffing and Key Personnel
Eric White, Owner/Project Manager (7+ years experience in facility services, recycling logistics, and project management) directs all operations and serves as the primary interface with the COR. Field crews consist of trained technicians with current OSHA 10-hour certifications and background checks where required. Staffing is scaled to the scope: Solicitation: Evaluation. Pricing Form: and Locations List – Pressure Washing Se. All personnel are W-2 employees or vetted subcontractors with documented qualifications.

## Past Performance
Rare Earth Ltd. brings directly relevant past performance totaling $529,200+ across 4 contracts. **Facility & Grounds – Integrated Recycling & Sustainability in Facility Maintenance**  - MCS Chainstore Maintenance, $50,000, Subcontractor, ongoing–ongoing. Managed logistics for large recycling flows; Coordinated multi-partner teams; Implemented Sustainability and Recycling Goals.

**Applicable Requirements:** Pricing Form: and Locations List – Pressure Washing Services; E-Verify enrollment required; Standard Form: 144.


---

## Pricing Narrative

Pricing for pressure washing is developed using a transparent cost-build methodology. Each line item includes direct labor (crew hours at burdened rates), materials and chemicals, fuel/mobilization, and allocated overhead (insurance, vehicle, administrative). A 20% margin target ensures financial sustainability while remaining competitive.

**Cost Basis by Service:**
- **pressure washing (commercial lot):** Labor: $270 (8 crew-hrs × burdened rate), Materials: $65, Fuel/mobilization: $23, Overhead: $75  - Cost floor: $510 (15% margin target)
- **pressure washing (deck patio):** Labor: $135 (4 crew-hrs × burdened rate), Materials: $65, Fuel/mobilization: $23, Overhead: $75  - Cost floor: $351 (15% margin target)

Factor insurance premium costs into overhead. Price competitively as lowest responsive bidder (eRFQ/IFB evaluation). Volume pricing and multi-year cost adjustments are available upon request.

sections.paragraph: Rare Earth Ltd. submits this proposal in response to DEPT OF DEFENSE's requirement for GROUNDS MAINTENANCE FORT STEWART/HAAF. As a certified DBE/SBA/Minority-Owned Small Business, Rare Earth brings proven capability in pressure washing, pressure washing with $529,200+ in past contract value. sections.title: Quality Control
sections.purpose: QA/QC framework, inspection checkpoints, and corrective action process.
sections.evaluatorQuestion: How does the offeror address Quality Control?
sections.paragraph: Rare Earth Ltd. implements a three-tier QC process: (1) pre-task planning with crew-specific checklists derived from the PWS, (2) in-progress inspection by the crew lead at defined checkpoints, and (3) post-task documentation including timestamped, GPS-tagged photos of completed work. Nonconformances trigger a corrective action cycle: identify deficiency → root cause → corrective action → COR notification → re-inspection within 24 hours. Customer satisfaction is verified through post-service follow-up.
